title: Your First App: Hello World
description: Create a new Expo project and run it on your device or simulator.

slides.title: Creating a New Project 🏗️
slides.content:
# `expo init`

In your terminal, navigate to the folder where you keep your projects and run:

```bash
expo init MyFirstApp
```

You'll be prompted to choose a template:
* **blank** – minimal setup, just an `App.js` file.
* **tabs** – includes a tab navigation example.

Select **blank** (the managed workflow).

slides.title: Project Structure 📂
slides.content:
## What's Inside?

After creation, open the `MyFirstApp` folder. Here are the key files:

* `App.js` – the root component of your app. This is where you'll start coding.
* `app.json` – configuration for your app (name, icon, splash screen, etc.).
* `package.json` – lists dependencies and scripts.
* `node_modules/` – all installed packages (don't touch this!).

slides.title: Hello World in React Native
Running the App 🚀
slides.content:
## `expo start`

1. In the terminal, make sure you're inside `MyFirstApp`.
2. Run `expo start` (or `npm start`).
3. A QR code will appear in the terminal and a new browser tab will open with the Metro bundler.

### On a physical device
* Install the **Expo Go** app from the App Store (iOS) or Google Play (Android).
* Scan the QR code with your phone's camera (iOS) or the Expo Go app (Android).

### On an emulator
* Press `i` to open iOS simulator (macOS only) or `a` to open Android emulator (requires Android Studio setup).

quizData.question: Which command creates a new Expo project?
quizData.explanation: `expo init` is the correct command to scaffold a new Expo project.

slides.title: Common Pitfall ⚠️
slides.content: If you see an error like `Unable to resolve module`, try clearing the cache with `expo start -c`.
